value_chain_position: AI GPU/accelerator chip design (MI series) + CPU for servers — the primary credible alternative to NVDA in AI compute with growing data-center CPU share.
relative_comps: AMD is the clearest NVDA challenger but trails decisively on software (ROCM vs CUDA) and installed base; its MI300X has found traction with Microsoft Azure and Meta for inference workloads. Data-center revenue grew 69% YoY most recently but remains ~10% of NVDA's scale. AMD's CPU franchise (EPYC) is a genuine market-share gainer vs Intel in server CPUs. AMD trades at ~40x forward earnings — similar to NVDA despite much smaller AI scale — pricing in a share-gain narrative that is still unproven at hyperscaler training workloads.
